88: **Australia**, level on 4 points with one other team from their group (position not yet separated), face **Egypt**, also level on 5 points with one other team (position not yet separated), at 11:00 AM PT at AT&T Stadium in Dallas — Egypt's first-ever appearance in a knockout match at the finals. Egypt's injury crisis is severe: captain Mohamed Salah is a major doubt with a hamstring strain suffered against Iran, Ahmed Fattouh is effectively ruled out with a hamstring tear, and Mohamed Abdelmonem carries ankle bruising; Salah has scored once and assisted twice across the group stage. Australia have never won a knockout match at the finals and arrive with Mathew Leckie and Jacob Italiano already ruled out of the tournament; the winner advances to the Round of 16 in Atlanta to face **Argentina** or **Cabo Verde**.
86: **Argentina**, 1st in **Group J** on 9 points after winning all three group games — scoring eight goals in the process — meet **Cabo Verde**, 2nd in **Group H** on 3 points, at 3:00 PM PT at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami, a tournament debut for the Blue Sharks, who became the smallest African nation to reach the knockouts. Cabo Verde midfielder Telmo Arcanjo is out with a hamstring injury, while Jamiro Monteiro and Kevin Lenini both recovered from knocks to feature in the group stage; goalkeeper Vozinha, who kept clean sheets against **Spain** and **Saudi Arabia**, is the man standing between the debutants and Lionel Messi, who has already scored six goals in three group appearances. The winner faces **Australia** or **Egypt** in the Round of 16 in Atlanta on July 7.
87: **Colombia**, 1st in **Group K** on 7 points, face **Ghana**, 3rd in **Group L** on 4 points, at 6:30 PM PT at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City — the first-ever meeting between the two nations at the finals. Ghana are without talisman Mohammed Kudus (pre-tournament injury) and Thomas Partey's availability is being monitored by manager Carlos Queiroz, while Antoine Semenyo carries a knock from the Croatia defeat; Colombia's Daniel Muñoz leads their scoring charts with two goals from right-back after being rested against **Portugal**, and Luis Suárez has shaken off a fitness concern to return to contention. The winner faces either **Switzerland** or **Algeria** in the Round of 16.
84: **Spain**, 1st in **Group H** on 7 points and yet to concede a single goal in three group games, face **Austria** — level on 4 points with one other team, their position not yet separated — at 12:00 PM PT at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. Spain are without wingers Nico Williams (groin) and Yeremy Pino (collarbone), both confirmed out after the 1-0 win over **Uruguay** via Alex Baena's decisive goal, while Víctor Muñoz (muscle) is a fitness doubt having played zero minutes all tournament; Lamine Yamal — managed to 141 minutes across the group stage following an April hamstring tear — is now said to be available for as long as required. Austria carry a clean injury table, with David Alaba cleared after a fitness scare and Marko Arnautović fit despite a knee knock in the **Algeria** thriller; the two nations have met just once at the finals, a 2-1 Austria win in 1978, with the winner here advancing to face **Portugal** or **Croatia** in the Round of 16 in Dallas.
83: **Portugal**, 2nd in **Group K** on 5 points, meet **Croatia**, 2nd in **Group L** on 6 points, at 4:00 PM PT at BMO Field in Toronto — the first-ever knockout meeting between these two nations at the finals. Both squads arrive with clean injury lists; Portugal's João Neves is set to return to the starting midfield alongside Vitinha after being rested for the 0-0 draw with **Colombia**, while Zlatko Dalić is expected to recall Joško Gvardiol at left-back after dropping him for the **Ghana** match, allowing Ivan Perišić to move further forward. Cristiano Ronaldo, in his sixth and presumed final appearance at the finals, has scored twice this tournament — both against **Uzbekistan** — while 40-year-old Modric remains **Croatia**'s primary midfield conductor; **Croatia** have reached the semi-finals or beyond at each of their last three tournament appearances (runners-up 2018, third 2022), and the winner faces the **Spain**–**Austria** winner in the Round of 16.
85: **Switzerland**, 1st in **Group B** on 7 points and unbeaten through the group stage, meet **Algeria** — level on 4 points with one other team, their position not yet separated — at 8:00 PM PT at BC Place in Vancouver, the first-ever competitive meeting between the two nations. Algeria forward Mohamed El Amine Amoura (hamstring) is confirmed out, leaving Riyad Mahrez — who scored twice against **Austria** — as their primary attacking threat; manager Vladimir Petković previously spent seven years coaching **Switzerland** before handing the role to Murat Yakin in 2021. Switzerland's Silvan Widmer missed a training session with a hip concern and carries a questionable fitness tag; a Swiss victory would mark their fourth consecutive Round of 16 appearance, while Algeria are only the second time in their history in the knockouts (the first came in 2014, when they lost to eventual winners Germany). The winner faces **Colombia** or **Ghana** in the Round of 16.
